system_prompt:
<role>
You are a senior web strategist at a digital agency (like EIGHT25MEDIA) specializing in SEO, conversion optimization, content strategy, and UX for marketing websites.
You evaluate pages the way an agency would before a client proposal — looking for concrete wins, not theoretical advice.
You are precise, analytical, and data-driven.
</role>

<constraints>
## Analysis Constraints
1. Analyze ONLY using the provided metrics and content — do not use external knowledge about the specific website.
2. You MUST reference specific metrics by name and value in every finding.
3. Check for Heading Hierarchy Violations: H3 before any H2, missing H1, multiple H1s, level skips.
4. Evaluate meta title length (ideal: 50–60 chars) and meta description length (ideal: 120–160 chars).
5. Consider rich visual media holistically: SVGs, CSS animations, video embeds, Lottie, canvas/WebGL, or 3D elements.
6. Assess technical SEO signals: viewport meta, canonical URL, robots meta, Open Graph tags, Twitter Card, structured data.
7. Evaluate CTA quality: quantity relative to page length, action-oriented language, placement signals.
8. For content depth: topical coverage, subheadings, internal/external link strategy, E-E-A-T signals.
9. Do NOT make generic statements — every claim must cite a metric or content excerpt.
10. Scores must be integers from 1 to 10.

## Recommendation Constraints
11. Generate exactly 3 to 5 recommendations in the "recommendations" array.
12. Every recommendation MUST be directly grounded in your analysis findings.
13. Prioritize by impact: priority 1 = highest impact must-fix; priority 2 = significant improvement; priority 3 = nice-to-have.
14. The "grounded_metric" field must cite the exact data point.
15. The "action" field must be a specific, implementable step.
16. The "expected_impact" field must describe the concrete expected outcome.
17. Focus recommendations on the lowest-scoring categories first.
</constraints>

<scoring_rubric>
- 9-10: Exceptional — best practices exceeded
- 7-8: Good — solid foundation with minor improvements possible
- 5-6: Average — functional but missing key optimizations
- 3-4: Below average — significant gaps
- 1-2: Critical — fundamental issues needing immediate attention
</scoring_rubric>
user_prompt: ## Extracted Page Metrics (Deterministic — scraped from HTML) ### Content Metrics - Word Count: 421 - CTA Count: 3 - Internal Links: 15 - External Links: 2 ### Image & Visual Media - Total Images (): 5 - Images Missing Alt Text: 2 (40.0%) - Images with Decorative Alt (alt=""): 0 - Rich Visual Media: None detected ### Technical SEO - Meta Title: Welcome to Acme Corp (20 chars — ideal: 50-60) - Meta Description: Acme Corp is the leading provider of widgets. (46 chars — ideal: 120-160) - Viewport Meta: Present - Canonical URL: MISSING - Robots Meta: Not set - Open Graph Tags: MISSING - Twitter Card: MISSING - Structured Data (JSON-LD): None ### Heading Structure - Heading Counts: {"h1": 0, "h2": 2, "h3": 1, "h4": 0, "h5": 0, "h6": 0} ## Heading Hierarchy (order as found in HTML) [["H2", "About Us"], ["H3", "Our Team"], ["H2", "Pricing"]] ## Page Content (visible text excerpt) Welcome to Acme Corp... Perform a complete website audit in two parts: **PART 1 — Analysis**: Evaluate the page across all five categories (structure, messaging, CTAs, content depth, UX). **PART 2 — Recommendations**: Based on your analysis, generate 3-5 prioritized recommendations. Return everything as a single structured JSON object matching the schema.
raw_response:
{
  "structure_score": 3,
  "messaging_score": 7,
  "cta_score": 6,
  "content_depth_score": 5,
  "ux_score": 4,
  "overall_score": 5,
  "structure_analysis": {
    "score": 3,
    "findings": "The page lacks an H1 tag entirely — heading hierarchy starts with H2 ('About Us'). This is a critical SEO violation. Meta title is only 20 chars (ideal: 50-60) and meta description is 46 chars (ideal: 120-160), both severely undersized. No canonical URL, Open Graph, or Twitter Card meta tags are present. No structured data (JSON-LD) detected.",
    "evidence": "Heading counts: h1=0, h2=2, h3=1. Meta title: 20 chars. Meta description: 46 chars. Canonical: MISSING. OG tags: MISSING."
  },
  "messaging_analysis": {
    "score": 7,
    "findings": "Messaging is reasonably clear with identifiable value proposition, but the meta description ('Acme Corp is the leading provider of widgets') is generic and doesn't differentiate from competitors.",
    "evidence": "Word Count: 421. Meta description: 'Acme Corp is the leading provider of widgets.'"
  },
  "cta_analysis": {
    "score": 6,
    "findings": "3 CTAs detected for a 421-word page is adequate density. However, without explicit placement signals, it's unclear if any appear above the fold.",
    "evidence": "CTA Count: 3. Word Count: 421."
  },
  "content_depth_analysis": {
    "score": 5,
    "findings": "Content is thin at 421 words — below the 600-word SEO threshold for competitive ranking. Only 2 external links suggest limited topical authority signals. The page has 15 internal links which is positive for site architecture.",
    "evidence": "Word Count: 421. External Links: 2. Internal Links: 15."
  },
  "ux_analysis": {
    "score": 4,
    "findings": "Significant accessibility concern: 2 out of 5 images (40%) are missing alt text entirely. Viewport meta is present (mobile-ready). No rich visual media detected — page relies solely on traditional images.",
    "evidence": "Images Missing Alt: 2 (40%). Viewport: Present. Rich Media: None."
  },
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "category": "seo",
      "title": "Add H1 tag and fix heading hierarchy",
      "description": "The page has zero H1 tags. The heading hierarchy starts at H2 ('About Us'). Search engines rely heavily on H1 to understand page topic and relevance.",
      "grounded_metric": "h1: 0 — heading hierarchy starts with H2",
      "action": "Add a single H1 tag at the top of the page with the primary keyword. Ensure heading levels descend properly: H1 → H2 → H3.",
      "expected_impact": "Improves search engine understanding of page topic. H1 is a top-3 on-page SEO factor — can improve rankings for target keywords."
    },
    {
      "priority": 1,
      "category": "seo",
      "title": "Expand meta title and description to ideal lengths",
      "description": "Meta title is 20 chars (ideal: 50-60) and meta description is 46 chars (ideal: 120-160). Both are severely undersized, resulting in poor SERP appearance and lower click-through rates.",
      "grounded_metric": "Meta title: 20 chars (ideal 50-60). Meta description: 46 chars (ideal 120-160)",
      "action": "Rewrite meta title to 50-60 chars including primary keyword. Expand meta description to 120-160 chars with a compelling value proposition and call to action.",
      "expected_impact": "Increases CTR in search results by 15-30%. Better SERP snippet presentation."
    },
    {
      "priority": 2,
      "category": "ux",
      "title": "Add alt text to all images",
      "description": "2 out of 5 images (40%) are completely missing alt text. This is both an accessibility violation (WCAG 2.1) and a missed SEO opportunity for image search.",
      "grounded_metric": "2 images missing alt text (40% of total)",
      "action": "Audit all 5 images. Add descriptive alt text to the 2 missing ones. Use alt=\"\" only for purely decorative images.",
      "expected_impact": "Improves accessibility compliance and image search visibility. Resolves WCAG 2.1 Level A violation."
    },
    {
      "priority": 2,
      "category": "seo",
      "title": "Add canonical URL, Open Graph, and Twitter Card meta tags",
      "description": "No canonical URL, Open Graph, or Twitter Card meta tags are present. This hurts social sharing previews and can cause duplicate content issues.",
      "grounded_metric": "Canonical: MISSING. Open Graph: MISSING. Twitter Card: MISSING",
      "action": "Add <link rel='canonical'> pointing to the preferred URL. Add og:title, og:description, og:image, and twitter:card meta tags.",
      "expected_impact": "Prevents duplicate content penalties. Enables rich social media previews when shared on Facebook, Twitter, LinkedIn."
    },
    {
      "priority": 3,
      "category": "content",
      "title": "Expand content depth beyond 600 words",
      "description": "At 421 words, the page falls below the 600-word threshold commonly needed for competitive ranking on informational queries.",
      "grounded_metric": "Word Count: 421 (below 600-word SEO threshold)",
      "action": "Add 200-400 words expanding on the 'About Us' and 'Pricing' sections. Include relevant subheadings (H2/H3) and internal links to related pages.",
      "expected_impact": "Improves topical depth signals for search engines. Longer content correlates with higher rankings for informational queries."
    }
  ]
}

structure_analysis.findings: The page lacks an H1 tag entirely — heading hierarchy starts with H2 ('About Us'). This is a critical SEO violation. Meta title is only 20 chars (ideal: 50-60) and meta description is 46 chars (ideal: 120-160), both severely undersized. No canonical URL, Open Graph, or Twitter Card meta tags are present.
